    "R1.4": "The section claims novelty without sufficient comparison to prior work on calibration in LLMs. While it mentions some related work, it doesn't adequately demonstrate the distinctiveness of its approach.",
    "R1.7": "There are a few citations missing, particularly regarding the specific techniques used for extracting confidence scores and the background on RLHF overconfidence.",
    "RX.1": "The text does not explicitly discuss uncertainty in the model's outputs or acknowledge any limitations in its calibration.",
    "RX.3": "The section generally stays within the scope of discussing a reinforcement learning approach to calibrate confidence in language models.",
    "RX.4": "No harmful content or regressions are identified in the text.",
    "RX.5": "The text does not provide details on how the model's outputs are generated or how they relate to the training data."
  },

rationale.R1.4: The section claims novelty without sufficient comparison to prior work on calibration in LLMs. While it mentions some related work, it doesn't adequately demonstrate the distinctiveness of its approach.
rationale.R1.7: There are a few citations missing, particularly regarding the specific techniques used for extracting confidence scores and the background on RLHF overconfidence.
rationale.RX.1: The text does not explicitly discuss uncertainty in the model's outputs or acknowledge any limitations in its calibration.
rationale.RX.3: The section generally stays within the scope of discussing a reinforcement learning approach to calibrate confidence in language models.
rationale.RX.4: No harmful content or regressions are identified in the text.
rationale.RX.5: The text does not provide details on how the model's outputs are generated or how they relate to the training data.
